mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns show no statistical correlation with FEDFUNDS changes over a 5-year rolling window, or Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 6.5 percent, labor market slack signals imminent demand contraction and earnings downgrades, driving negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns remain positive in 70 percent of instances where UNRATE exceeds 6.5 percent, or the signal loses predictive power during recession onset.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal spending and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and investment demand, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns when RSAFS and HOUST diverge, or out-of-sample Sharpe ratio drops below 0.5 over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase reflects capacity utilization improvements and margin expansion, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over one month.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration generates negative or flat 20-day returns during post-shock recovery periods, or the mechanism reverses direction when capacity utilization exceeds 85 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid consumer price inflation above 0.4 percent monthly forces central bank tightening expectations, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns remain positive in 65 percent of cases when CPIAUCSL MoM exceeds 0.4 percent, or the signal loses significance during supply-driven inflation episodes.

mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms labor market tightening and wage growth, supporting corporate revenue expansion and positive S&P 500 returns over three months.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal predicts negative 60-day returns when wage growth outpaces productivity, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth directly increases corporate earnings expectations and equity risk premium compression, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: GDPC1 quarterly changes show zero correlation with 20-day SPX returns over a 10-year sample, or Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all market conditions.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a late-cycle expansion signals capacity constraints and margin compression, driving negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines produce positive 5-day returns during late-cycle expansions, or the signal fails to predict direction in 70 percent of observed instances.
